rationale.R1.1: The section makes two competing primary-contribution claims: paragraph 1 names the dialogue-based decomposition protocol as 'the principal scientific advance,' while the final paragraph reframes the dataset/benchmark as the true value. Reconstructing which contribution claim the work actually stands on is central to any review.
rationale.R1.2: A direct architectural contradiction spans paragraphs 2 and 3: paragraph 2 introduces 'a central orchestrator agent' that 'issues the final action token,' while paragraph 3 asserts 'a fully decentralized peer-to-peer channel with no central coordinator.' These claims are mutually exclusive and one invalidates experimental description. This is load-bearing; the inconsistency must be resolved for the section to be coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The section claims the protocol 'generalizes across embodiments and task families' but the entire evaluation is a single dual-arm Franka platform doing tabletop pick-and-place. The generalization claim is not supported by the reported evidence and requires flagging.
rationale.R1.6: Writing review is a plausible primary intent of 'review this section,' making sentence- and paragraph-level quality central rather than peripheral, pending clarification.
